questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Why does a loudspeaker cone move backwards and forwards?
options: The permanent magnet becomes hotter and pushes the cone outwards only.
The cone stores charge and releases it mechanically.
The wire stops producing a magnetic field whenever sound is needed.
The current in the coil changes direction, so the force on the coil also changes direction.
questions.correctAnswer: The current in the coil changes direction, so the force on the coil also changes direction.
questions.explanation: Alternating current changes direction, so the motor effect pushes the coil in opposite directions and the cone vibrates.
questions.senNote: Alternating current changes direction, so the motor effect pushes the coil in opposite directions and the cone vibrates.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: What is electromagnetic induction?
options: A current is made by heating a wire until it glows.
A magnetic field is destroyed when iron is placed near it.
A conductor becomes a permanent magnet whenever a battery is removed.
A potential difference is produced when a conductor cuts through magnetic field lines.
questions.correctAnswer: A potential difference is produced when a conductor cuts through magnetic field lines.
questions.explanation: Electromagnetic induction happens when a conductor experiences a changing magnetic field.
questions.senNote: Electromagnetic induction happens when a conductor experiences a changing magnetic field.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe how to strengthen an electromagnet
questions.question: How can the strength of an electromagnet be increased?
options: Use fewer turns of wire in the coil.
Remove the iron core from the centre.
Reduce the number of cells to lower the current.
Increase the current in the coil.
questions.correctAnswer: Increase the current in the coil.
questions.explanation: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.
questions.senNote: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.

questions.senNote: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe how to strengthen an electromagnet
questions.question: How can the strength of an electromagnet be increased? Choose the best answer.
options: Increase the current in the coil.
Use fewer turns of wire in the coil.
Remove the iron core from the centre.
Reduce the number of cells to lower the current.
questions.correctAnswer: Increase the current in the coil.
questions.explanation: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.
questions.senNote: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe how to strengthen an electromagnet
questions.question: How can the strength of an electromagnet be increased? Choose the best answer.
options: Use fewer turns of wire in the coil.
Remove the iron core from the centre.
Reduce the number of cells to lower the current.
Increase the current in the coil.
questions.correctAnswer: Increase the current in the coil.
questions.explanation: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.
questions.senNote: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: What causes the motor effect?
options: A stationary conductor produces no magnetic field and so creates gravity.
A magnetic field always stops current flowing in a conductor.
The conductor gains mass when current passes through it.
A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
questions.correctAnswer: A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.
questions.senNote: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain why an iron core strengthens a solenoid
questions.question: Why does adding an iron core to a solenoid make the field stronger?
options: The core stops the current from flowing through the coil.
The core removes all magnetic poles from the coil.
The core reduces the number of turns in the solenoid.
The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
questions.correctAnswer: The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.
questions.senNote: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.
